The Region and Business Unit records are filtered for the entries in the "USER_ACCESS_FILTERS" table that match the SESSION_USER (DEMOUSER_SSO). The SQL Expression can be modified to accommodate various scenarios, given that it remains a valid WHERE statement.

 

5.      Grant Access to a large audience

The advantages of implementing this configuration become most evident when sharing the SAP Analytics Cloud story with a broad audience. As of now, only the user DEMOUSER_SSO is granted access to the story. To facilitate the onboarding of additional users with personalized data access, these three steps provide guidance:

  • Each user granted access to the story requires a mapping to an SAP HANA Cloud user through the configured SAML provider. The role of the Analytic Privilege must be assigned to the SAP HANA Cloud user. It can be considered whether the mapping should be a straightforward one-to-one relationship, with each SAP Analytics Cloud user corresponding to one SAP HANA Cloud user, or a many-to-one arrangement, where multiple SAP Analytics Cloud users are linked to a single SAP HANA Cloud user. The first option is advisable for projects with a moderate user count and diverse filtering requirements, while the latter is better suited for scenarios with a high number of users and minimal filtering combinations.
  • Each SAP HANA Cloud user must have a minimum of one entry in the "USER_ACCESS_FILTERS" table. The username inputted into this table must precisely correspond with the SAP HANA Cloud user's name.
  • The SAP Analytics Cloud Model and Story must be shared with the respective SAP Analytics Cloud user.

Adding multiple users in one batch is possible by executing an SQL script in either the HANA Cloud Cockpit or the SAP HANA Database Explorer. Through the SQL script, you not only add users but also establish a connection to the identity provider and assign the Analytic Privilege role to each user.

 

6.      Conclusion

The underlying scenario demonstrates the feasibility of implementing user-based data access control seamlessly between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP HANA Cloud accessing live data. It is worth emphasizing that users intending to execute the outlined steps require access to the root HDI container with the tables referenced in the Calculation view, as well as administrative access to the SAP HANA Cloud Cockpit. Overall, this integration use case contributes to an enhanced, secure, and tailored analytics experience within the SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Q1 2024
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the new features and enhancements of the SAP Analytics Cloud Q1 2024 Quarterly Release (QRC).

Join our SAP Product Experts in the video below as they demonstrate some of the features highlighted in Q1 2024.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.395 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.91 for SAP universe (UNX)
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Important: With every quarterly release update, there are required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud via SNOTES. This will ensure that the SAP BW back and font ends are in sync. To learn which SNOTES are required for implementation, refer to the XML-File attached in SAP Note 2541557.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Service Marketplace.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • External hyperlink list for trusted domains | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Secondary CMEK rotation | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Switching to the advanced calculation engine in SAP Profitability and Performance Management | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Notification only - Phasing out scheduled model export to File Repo
  • Augmented Analytics
    • Just ask: next-generation natural language query (NLQ) powered by AI | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Blog)
    • Predictive Planning – ability to understand individual forecasts | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Predictive planning – automation of what-if simulation | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Business Content Delivery
    • Business content packages for consensus net revenue planning, financial and sales planning, and process insights | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Data Center Availability
    • Data-center availability in Switzerland with EU access through Microsoft Azure | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Data Exploration
    • Data Analyzer
      • Create calculated measures (formula editor) | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • Export: option in the dialog to print repetitive members | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW): dimension groups support in the builder panel | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • URL API support of insights | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Data Integration
    • Data Export API – support for public dimensions | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Live SAP BW – improved support of time-dependent hierarchies | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Standard Unit Data Export API
    • Improve data mnotive job readiness check
  • Data Modeling
    • Ability to create new models with data uploads | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • New model – ability to transform data in the fact table | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • New model – dimensions without master data | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • New Model – Disaggregation based on another measure | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • To-date formulas in model calculations | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Visual representation of relationships between objects within the calculation workspace | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Enterprise Planning
    • Advanced Formulas - calculated member support | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Advanced Formulas – variable member support for version dimension | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data Actions - toggle ID or Description for members | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data Actions – cross-model copy with analytic models as a source | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data Actions – manual migration option for existing allocations | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Plan Entry – Fluid Data Entry support for SAP BPC live | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Plan Process Management - public APIs for use in business process automation for SAP BTP | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Plan Process Management – synchronizing calendar events with Office 365 | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Version Management - display invalid fact records during publishing | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Undo/Redo for Editing BPC Planning Sequence Trigger Widget
  • Microsoft Office Integration
    • Continuing member selector updates in Microsoft Excel – Select a Range | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Formatting of a static report in Microsoft Excel | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Support for a new cell-based custom formula in Microsoft Excel | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Updates to the designer pane header in Microsoft Excel | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Story Design
    • Applying story, page, local, and linked analysis filter with internal (page or story) hyperlinks | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Initial Release of Lite Viewer | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Dynamic text support for geo-local filters | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Enable Export in the Background option for Optimized Story Experience
    • Optimized story experience – Export to Microsoft PowerPoint | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Table – version sort | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Time calculations for a live SAP BW | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Video)
    • Variable support – saving a story without preselecting variables | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Variance as a waterfall chart (for bar-column and horizontal waterfall) | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Story Calculations
      • Aggregations in Cross Calculations
      • Add Calculation from Available Objects Panel
  • Story Extensibility
    • Customizing toolbar for mobile devices | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Support for bookmarking custom widgets | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Supporting script translation using a text pool | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Supporting unbooked members for input control script API | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • User Collaboration
    • Commenting in models with role-based data security applied | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Enabling data point comments on restricted cross calculations with a new model | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Removal of the “no end date” option when scheduling a publication story | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Sending notifications to users when the schedule fails or is partially successful | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Support for data point comments when dimension properties are used in a table | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Include Formatting Option Now Available for Scheduling Publication to CSV Format

 

Administration & Service Management

 

External hyperlink list for trusted domains

Users can now enable a warning message when being redirected to an external URL. Admins can create a list of trust domains that are exempt from the warning. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Switching to the advanced calculation engine in SAP Profitability and Performance Management
  • Navigate between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Profitability and Performance Management using the product switcher menu in the top right of the shell bar navigation menu
  • Log in once and access the service again without re-authenticating

Note: When selecting advanced calculation engine in the switch menu for SAP Analytics Cloud, the shell bar navigation menu opens in a new browser tab showing the entry page for SAP Profitability and Performance Management. The URL for this is defined in the Link my Tenants section of the system administration page for the tenant. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Business Content Delivery

 

Business content packages for consensus net revenue planning, financial and sales planning, and process insights

Reduce the time spent implementing SAP Analytics Cloud by adopting new, prebuilt data models and stories.

Updated packages for industries and lines of business for Q1 2024 include: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

  • New industry and line-of-business content packages:
    • Security Recommendation dashboard
    • Portfolio financial planning for SAP S/4HANA
    • Consensus net revenue planning
    • Strategic workforce planning
  • Updated industry and line-of-business content packages:
    • Public sector management KPI reporting for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
    • Public sector management planning
    • Commercial planning
    • Cross-model functionality for integrated financial planning for SAP S/4HANA
    • Group financial planning for SAP S/4HANA
    • Integrated financial planning for SAP S/4HANA
    • Extended planning and analysis (XP&A) and human experience management (HXM)
    • Enterprise asset management and plant maintenance analysis for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Learn more about SAP Analytics Business Content here.

 

Data Center Availability

 

Data-center availability in Switzerland with EU access through Microsoft Azure

Customer now have more choices in data center locations and hyperscaler platforms through support for deployment on Microsoft Azure in Switzerland. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Data Integration

 

Data Export API – support for public dimensions
  • Adding a new namespace exposing public dimensions
  • Supporting two-legged and three-legged OAuth protocols

Note: Available on Cloud Foundry tenants. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Improve data motive job readiness check

An enhancement has been made to the data management UI for refreshing import jobs of a model in target tenants. See this SAP knowledge base article for more details. 

 

Enterprise Planning

 

Data Actions – cross-model copy with analytic models as a source

Ability to read data from an analytic (non-planning) model of SAP Analytics Cloud as a source model when using a cross-model copy operation. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

 

Microsoft Office Integration

 

Continuing member selector updates in Microsoft Excel – Select a Range

Expanded member-selector functionality in Excel includes:

  • Select a range of member to define them a filter.

This feature will be available only when the members will be visible in "flat list mode" in the Member selector. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

Please note this is a preview and will be released later in Q1 2024. This feature has been postponed to Q2 QRC.

 

Formatting of a static report in Microsoft Excel
  • New option available for tables, allowing users to deactivate the default table formatting and apply Excel style
  • When deactivating the default table formatting, user ability to add Excel formatting to the add-in table and to maintain the formatting upon “refresh” action
  • User ability to create highly customized formatting for static tables; however, this formatting will not support expand and collapse actions

(SAP Road Map Explorer)

This feature is available as of Jan 31, 2024.

 

Updates to the designer pane header in Microsoft Excel 
  • Display of the detailed content of a selected object in a sheet, through the designer pane header, which enables the user to make changes to the object, such as tables, data action triggers, or data sources used for formulas
  • Additional updates to the header to enrich the information and the navigation capabilities:
    • Enrichment of the information within the header with details regarding the object (table, data action trigger, or data source alias for formulas) shown within the sheet

(SAP Road Map Explorer)

This feature is available as of Jan 31, 2024.

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. For more details on these features, please visit the SAP Analytics Cloud Help Portal. You can also visit the SAP Road Map Explorer to see the upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.03
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.03 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q2 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

 

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.397 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Service Marketplace.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Central control over user profile settings for administrators | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Augmented Analytics
    • Predictive planning – making prediction intervals visible in stories | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Data Integration
    • Support x.509 certificates for SAP Integrated Business Planning connection type
  • Enterprise Planning
    • Advanced Formulas - using the link function with analytic models as a source | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Multi Actions – copy external API URL from detailed dialog | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Plan Process Management – extended APIs for stories and analytical applications | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data Clearance in the Cross-Model Copy Step of Data Actions
  • Microsoft Office Integration
    • Add-in for Microsoft Excel
      • Design Pane Header Re-design | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • Formatting of a static report in Microsoft Excel | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Story Design
    • New Formatting Options in Theme Preferences
    • Wild Card Filters (excludes SAP BW and SAP IBP)
  • Story Extensibility
    • Data Change Insights Burst Mode Support
    • Integrate Numeric Points in SAP Mobile Start via REST API

 

Administration and Service Management

 

Central control over user profile settings for administrators

Administrators can now centrally control the default user profile settings via the global user preference settings. Changes can be applied to new users only or to all users. This eliminates the manual effort for a user to configure their own language, date formatting, number formatting and email notifications.  (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Augmented Analytics

 

Predictive planning – making prediction intervals visible in stories

When using predictive planning, users can now save the prediction interval alongside the predictive forecasts to dedicated planning versions. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Data Integration

 

Support x.509 certificates for SAP Integrated Business Planning connection type

With this release, we are happy to announce the support of x.509 client certificate authentication for acquired connections for SAP Integrated Business Planning connection. This provides a new, secure authentication method for SAP IBP.

 

Enterprise Planning

 

Advanced Formulas - using the link function with analytic models as a source

Users can now use the link function within an advanced formula script with analytic models. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

For example: DATA() = LINK([<Analytic Model>], [d/Dimension] = "member", …)

 

Multi Actions – copy external API URL from detailed dialog

In the Multi Action settings dialog for a multi action with “Allow External API Access” enabled, users can now choose “Show Details” and copy “Multi Action ID”, “Trigger API URL”, or “Polling API URL” of this multi action to paste into an API request sent by an external application. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Plan Process Management – extended APIs for stories and analytical applications

New calendar API’s have been added in the optimized story experience and analytics designer. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

  • The createCompositeTask API now supports properties such as contextFilters, reminders, parentId, owners and dependencies. You can use the createProcess API to create a calendar process with similar properties as the createCompositeTask API.
  • You can use the sendImmediateReminder API to send an immediate reminder to the assignees with an optional message.
  • You can use APIs to update a calendar event, such as its start and end date, progress, owner, assignee, reminder, context filters.
  • You can use APIs to get more properties of a calendar event, such as its child events, dependencies, owners, context filters and reminders.
  • You can use the reopen API to reopen a calendar event.
  • The following APIs are deprecated: getCalendarTaskById, getRelatedTaskIds, getCurrentTask. Use getCalendarEventById, getRelatedEventIds, getCurrentEvent respectively instead.

 

Data Clearance in the Cross-Model Copy Step of Data Actions

Previously, when configuring a cross-model copy step in data actions while in overwrite mode, cross-model copy steps with no source context filter would sometimes clear additional data. Now, cross-model copy steps being configured in overwrite mode clear only the intended data.

 

Microsoft Office Integration

 

Add-in for Microsoft Excel

The name of the add-in has changed from SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Office to SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Excel.

 

Design Pane Header Re-design

Updates have been made to the design pane header. Depending on the selected object, the designer panel header now shows an icon for table, formula or data action trigger and the follow information: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

  • The data source name and the table name for tables.
  • The data source name and the data source alias for formulas.
  • The label and the ID for data action trigger.

This feature is available to all customers as of January 31, 2024.

 

Formatting of a static report in Microsoft Excel 

Users now have the option to disable the default format when adding a new table. Disabling the default table formatting will keep Excel formatting after rendering. This provides users the ability to create highly customized formatting for reports based on native Excel standing formatting, including conditional formatting. However, this formatting will not support expand and collapse actions. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Story Design

 

New Formatting Options in Theme Preferences

We have introduced additional formatting options within the Theme Preferences to be able to create an IBCS-like “Theme”.

  • Scale Format (k, m, bn vs Thousands, Millions, and Billions)
  • Absolute vs Percentage Decimal Places
  • Currency Format (System Default, ISO Code, and Symbol)

Please note the following limitations:

  • Only available for charts and tables
  • Currency Format Formatting Option is limited to Theme Preferences
  • Option to Configure Decimal Places for Absolute vs Percentage is limited to Theme Preferences

 

Wild Card Filters (excludes SAP BW and SAP IBP)

For non-time based dimensions, we have renamed it from Filter by Range to Filter by Condition

There are new options that are available which includes a Text Match vs Wildcard

  • For Text Match vs Wildcard we support the deselection of members at View Time but NOT editing the individual statements
  • For Matches Pattern we support * (Matches any number of characters) and ? (Matches a single alphabet in a specific position)

There is a new section called “Restrictions” that will exclude values based on the conditions that are set which will be read only for the Viewer

  • Designer has an option to hide the Restrictions from the Viewer

Please note the following limitations:

  • This is not supported for BW or IBP
  • Hierarchies do not support base comparison operators (i.e. Between, Greater Than, and so on) and Matches Pattern
  • (No Value) member will not be found based on a text match as its not a real member in the backend
  • Matches Pattern is limited to ? and * operators

 

Story Extensibility

 

Data Change Insights Burst Mode Support

Users can now choose burst mode when subscribing to data change insights. In burst mode, story viewers receive data change insights based on their own authorizations, rather than the scheduler's authorization.

 
Integrate Numeric Points in SAP Mobile Start via REST API

Application developers can call a rest API by story ID and widget ID to access data from numeric chart widgets in optimized story experience. This will allow users to consume the widgets in SAP Mobile Start.

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases. 

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. And check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Leverage SAP Analytics Cloud File Server and Python to Automate Data Integration for External API
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) hasn't provided REST API connectivity. This blog can be treated as a Proof-of-Concept. It addresses the gay of importing data from external REST API and scheduling data updates based on REST API data source. The main configuration flow is to: convert the payload into a CSV file by Python > schedule the CSV file-generating process by Python and Task Scheduler > store the CSV file in the file server by Python > schedule import CSV file into SAC from the file server via SAC model data management.

Alternatively, SAP BTP Cloud Platform Integration also provides the capability to convert REST API into OData API.  It requires knowledge of customizing the entity in the edmx file.

In this Blog, I will take api/v1/audit/activities/exportActivities APIs for SAP Analytics Cloud as an example.

Step 1. Store data as CSV files from an API at regular intervals

 

 

import requests
import requests.auth
from datetime import datetime
import time
import sys
import csv
print(sys.executable)

def fetch_data():
    client = 'sb-<mockup>-65' #replace with the real client_id
    secret = '49-<mockup>p='   #replace with the real client_secret
    token_url = 'https://<mockup>/oauth/token' #replace with the real token_URL
    tenant_url = 'https://<mockup>'  #replace with real tenant_URL

    sess = requests.Session()
    basic_auth = requests.auth.HTTPBasicAuth(client, secret)

    resp = sess.get(token_url, params={'grant_type': 'client_credentials'}, auth=basic_auth)
    print('Step 1: Status', resp.status_code) 

    bearer_token = resp.json().get('access_token')

    resp = sess.get(
        f'{tenant_url}/ResourceRequestService/v1/ResourceRequests',
        headers={
            'Authorization': f'Bearer {bearer_token}',
            'x-sap-sac-custom-auth': 'true'
            'x-csrf-token': 'fetch', #add/remove headers as per application required
        }
    )

    print('Step 2: Status', resp.status_code)  

    if resp.status_code == 200:
        textList = [resp.text]
        textListFormatted = [i.replace('"', '') for i in textList]
        filename = 'sapTestFile.csv'
        with open(filename, 'w', encoding='UTF8') as f: #adapt to the filePath and fileName as expected, and data in the file gets updated every n seconds
            sapwriter = csv.writer(f)
            sapwriter.writerow(textListFormatted)
        print('Step 3: Data fetched and saved successfully')
    else:
        print(f'Step 3: Error {resp.status_code} - Unable to fetch data')

def main():
    while True:
        fetch_data()
        time.sleep(60)  # Sleep for 60 seconds, address the time as needed

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

 

 

Step 2. Schedule Python script on Windows Task Scheduler

After generating CSV file successfully based on the script in Step 1, then please refer to Execute Python Script on Schedule – Windows Task Scheduler to schedule the script in the background. Linus and Mac operation systems also have similar methods.

Step 3. Build import connection from file server to SAC

Refer to Allow Data Import and Model Export with a File Server and How to Configure File Server for importing data and exporting a model in SAP Analytics Cloud

The result can be expected:

The data in the existing file is overwritten every 60 seconds based on the last API call.

The file can be imported into SAC by scheduling via the file server.

 

-----------------------------------------------------###################--------------------------------------------------

Other user cases of the Python file: 

  1. Archive the activity log from SAP Analytics Cloud automatically
  2. Integrate 3rd party data sources that provide REST API endpoint

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.04
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.04 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q2 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

 

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.397 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Service Marketplace.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Enterprise Planning
    • Multi Actions - Support analytic models in Multi Action parameter | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Story Extensibility
    • Composite Enhancement: Support Responsive Layout | (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Multi Actions - Support analytic models in Multi Action parameter

Users can now apply analytic models to multi actions parameters and can use these parameters in data action steps and API steps. The parameter can also be used dynamically in story runtime. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Story Extensibility

 

Composite Enhancement: Support Responsive Layout

Story designers can now leverage composites in responsive pages in the optimized story experience. This is part of a larger composite enhancement that will be part of the Q2 quarterly release. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

Learn more about composites in this blog post by @jie_deng .

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases. 

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. And check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Unraveling the SAP DataSphere: Insights in bite-sized pieces
entry.content:
SAP DataSphere is the next generation of SAP Data Warehouse Cloud that supports enterprise databases. It provides an integrated environment for data entry, indexing, word modeling, cluster storage, and virtualization. Comprehensive data services erected on the SAP Business Technology platform to give all data professionals with easy and scalable access to critical business data. This enables organizations to simplify their data warehouse environments by processing data from SAP and third-party cloud and on-premises environments into a single, fully integrated cloud environment. This feature was introduced in 2019 and renamed in March 2023. Generating large amounts of data, SAP dataSphere offers advantages such as scalability, cost savings, accessibility and data security. In addition to SAP DataSphere, SAP Analytics Cloud provides advanced analytics, planning and forecasting capabilities and the ability to activate data for decision-making, and SAP Digital Boardroom is an add-on that provides decision makers with the most informed information.

 

 

Benefits of SAP DataSphere

  • A secure environment that supports many data application requirements, including real-time analytics, database access management, and data science (machine learning).
  • Space provides a secure modeling environment for multiple departments or use cases.
  • Connectivity to multiple data sources is guaranteed, including SAP and non-SAP clouds and data lakes.
  • No-code and low-code architecture to support custom modeling needs of business users.
  • Seamless integration with SAP Analytics Cloud, using Microsoft Excel and the O Data public API to support the use of other clients, tools and applications.
  • Multiple visualizations and robust data analysis and visualization.
  • Graphical effects and genetic analysis to visualize changes in data movement and other dependencies.
  • Spatial collaboration and geospatial sharing to combine files with external sources that support low security.
  • Metamodels and data models in SAP Business Warehouse and SAP SQL Data Warehouse implementations.

 

Will SAP DataSphere Replaces BW4HANA

 

For more information regarding this you can go through this blog.

Why Your Business Needs SAP Datasphere: A Technical Deep Dive

In the age of information overload, managing and utilizing data effectively is no longer a luxury, but a necessity. SAP Datasphere emerges as a powerful solution, offering a comprehensive data management platform built for the modern enterprise. But why exactly is it essential, and what technical aspects make it stand out? Let's dive deep into the reasons why your business needs SAP Datasphere:

1. Conquering the Data Silos:

Imagine a world where data flows freely, unconstrained by isolated systems and inconsistent formats. That's the magic of SAP Datasphere. It acts as a central hub, seamlessly integrating data from diverse sources, both SAP and non-SAP. This includes ERP systems, customer databases, social media feeds, IoT sensors, and more. Think of it as breaking down data silos and creating a unified landscape, where every piece of information contributes to the bigger picture.

2. Data Management Nirvana:

Integration is just the first step. SAP Datasphere empowers you to manage your data effectively with a robust set of tools:

  • Data Cataloging: No more data black boxes! Catalog your data assets for clear understanding and efficient discovery.
  • Semantic Modeling: Ensure consistency and context across systems by defining business rules and relationships between data elements.
  • Data Governance: Implement robust security and access controls to maintain data integrity and compliance.
  • Data Quality Management: Identify and correct errors, ensuring your data is reliable and trustworthy.

3. Flexibility Tailored to Your Needs:

No two businesses are alike, so SAP Datasphere offers a flexible architecture to suit your specific needs. Choose from various solutions:

  • Data Warehousing: Store and analyze massive historical data volumes for in-depth insights.
  • Data Federation: Access and analyze data from multiple sources without physically moving it, ideal for distributed environments.
  • Data Virtualization: Provide on-demand access to data without impacting performance, perfect for real-time analytics.

4. Democratizing Data Access:

Data shouldn't just be confined to data scientists. SAP Datasphere fosters a culture of data-driven decision-making by empowering everyone in your organization:

  • Self-Service Data Access: Business analysts, executives, and even non-technical users can explore and analyze data independently through intuitive tools.
  • Collaboration and Sharing: Facilitate collaboration and knowledge sharing across teams with secure data access controls.

5. A Foundation for Advanced Analytics:

SAP Datasphere isn't just about data management; it's a springboard for advanced analytics. It seamlessly integrates with SAP Analytics Cloud, allowing you to:

  • Transform data into actionable insights: Leverage powerful visualization and analytics tools to uncover hidden patterns and trends.
  • Predictive Analytics and Machine Learning: Utilize cutting-edge AI capabilities to forecast future outcomes and make data-driven decisions.

Technical Advantages:

  • Open Architecture: Integrates with various data sources and tools, offering flexibility and customization.
  • Cloud-Native Design: Scalability and agility to handle demanding data workloads.
  • Security and Compliance: Robust security features to ensure data privacy and regulatory compliance.
  • API-Driven Development: Enables automation and integration with existing workflows.

How SAP DataSphere is different from SAP Analytics Cloud

  • SAP DataSphere is for data storage, data governance, and data security. SAC is just a reporting tool with limited data management capabilities.
  • DataSphere is designed for long-term data storage and a centralized data management system, SAC aims to create an interactive dashboard and application used by end users.
  • DataSphere is the data source and SAC is the data consumer.
  • Both are seamlessly integrated but you will have to pay a separate subscription fee.
  • DataSphere provides ETL functionality and complex replication flows, but SAC does not.
  • DataSphere also provides advanced data classification and modeling options and SAC does not offer advanced modeling.
  • DataSphere has relatively high capacity and memory usage compared to SAC.
  • We can integrate the two solutions in both directions.
  • Using data sphere we can just preview the data, but we cannot build advanced data insights. SAC can also be used to do collaborative enterprise planning.

 

How can you integrate SAP DataSphere and SAP Analytics Cloud

Integration Methods:

  1. Live Data Connection:

    • SAP Datasphere as a Live Source in SAC:

      1. In SAC, navigate to Connections and select "Add Connection."
      2. Choose "Live Data" and then "SAP Datasphere."
      3. Enter connection details like hostname and port.
      4. Select specific data entities or tables to connect to.
      5. This allows real-time analysis of data directly from Datasphere within SAC.
    • Prefer this video.

    • SAC as a Live Source in Datasphere:

      1. Currently, direct live connections from SAC to Datasphere are not supported.

  2. Data Loading and Transfer:

    • Datasphere Data to SAC:

      1. Use Cloud Data Integration in Datasphere to create a data flow or replication task.
      2. Load data from Datasphere tables into SAC models or data sets.
      3. This enables historical analysis and transformation of data within SAC.

    • SAC Data to Datasphere:

      1. Use the OAuth client in SAC to connect to Datasphere.
      2. Develop scripts or tools to load data from SAC models or data sets into Datasphere.
      3. This allows further processing, enrichment, or archiving of data in Datasphere.

  3. Other Integration Options:

    • ODBC and JDBC Drivers: Connect to Datasphere via ODBC or JDBC drivers directly within SAC for ad-hoc analysis.
    • OData Services: Expose specific Datasphere entities as OData services accessible from SAC for data exploration.      

For integration you can prefer this tutorial or this blog.

entry.title: SAP分析云2024年第一季度更新介绍
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能 (BI)、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和数据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。

本博文介绍 SAP 分析云 2024 年第一季度的季度发布 (QRC) 的新功能和增强功能。

在链接的视频中，SAP 产品专家将向您展示 2024 年第一季度的部分重点功能。

系统和技术要求

尽管不是必需的，我们推荐您升级到最新的 SAP 分析云代理版本 1.0.395，以利用所有数据采集类型。

除以下情况外，您的代理需要 1.0.75 或以上版本：

  • SAP ERP/SQL 数据库版本 1.0.99
  • SAP Universe (UNX)版本 1.0.91
  • SAP Business Warehouse (BW)版本 1.0.353
  • SAP HANA View 版本 1.0.235
  • File Server 版本 1.0.248

更多详细信息，请参阅系统要求和技术先决条件。

每季度发布更新时，都需要通过 SNOTES 更新 SAP BW 与 SAP 分析云的集成。这将确保 SAP BW 的后端和前端同步。要了解实施时需要哪些 SNOTES，请参阅 SAP Note 2541557 中所附的 XML 文件。

有关将 SAP BW 与 SAP 分析云集成的受支持功能和所需更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2541557 以了解更多详细信息。

有关通过 SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 与 SAP 分析云来实时访问 Universe 时的受支持功能和所需更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2771921 以了解更多详细信息。

请从“Service Marketplace”下载最新版本的“简易部署工具箱”。

为避免下载旧版本并可能让用户面对 Cloud Connector 与 SAPJVM 或 Apache Tomcat 的绑定不当带来的漏洞，只有三个最新版本可供下载。

关于云代理的版本变更管理细节，请参考 SAP Note 3264839。

 

更新亮点

  • 系统和服务管理
    • 可信任域的外部超链接列表 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 二次 CMEK 轮换 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 切换到 SAP Profitability and Performance Management 中的高级计算引擎 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 仅发布变更通知 - 逐步停止计划将模型导出至文件库的功能副本
  • 增强分析
    • Just Ask：AI驱动的新一代自然语言查询（NLQ）|（SAP路线图浏览器）（博文）
    • 预测计划 - 了解单项预测的能力 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 假设模拟自动化 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
  • 业务内容交付
    • 用于共识净收入规划、财务和销售规划以及流程洞察的业务内容包 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 数据中心可用性
    • 数据中心位于瑞士，可通过 Microsoft Azure 接入欧盟网络 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 数据发掘
    • 数据分析器
      • 创建计算度量（公式编辑器）|（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 在导出对话框中设置重复维成员打印选项 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW)：生成器面板中的维组支持 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • 洞察的 URL API 支持 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 数据集成
    • 数据导出 API - 支持公共维 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 实时 SAP BW – 改进了对时间相关层次结构的支持 |（SAP 路线图浏览器)
    • 标准单位数据导出API
  • 数据建模
    • 通过上传数据创建新模型的能力 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 新模型 - 能够转换事实表中的数据 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 新模型 - 无主数据的维 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 新模型 - 根据另一个度量进行分解 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 模型计算中的 To-date 公式 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 计算工作区中对象之间关系的可视化表示 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 企业计划
    • 高级公式 – 支持计算成员 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 高级公式 - 版本维的变量成员支持 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 数据操作 - 切换成员的 ID 或描述 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 数据操作 - 以分析模型为源进行跨模型复制 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 数据操作 - 现有分配的手动迁移选项 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 计划输入 - SAP BPC 实时数据源的流式数据输入支持 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 计划流程管理 - 用于 SAP BTP 业务流程自动化的公用 API  |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 计划流程管理 - 与Office 365同步日历事件 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 版本管理 - 在发布过程中显示无效的事实记录 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 撤消/重做以编辑 BPC 计划序列触发器微件
  • Microsoft Office 集成
    • Microsoft Excel 中的成员选择器的持续更新 - 选择范围 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 在 Microsoft Excel 中设置静态报告的格式 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 支持 Microsoft Excel 中基于单元格的新自定义公式 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • Microsoft Excel 设计器窗格标题的更新 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 故事设计
    • 应用带有内部（页面或故事）超链接的故事、页面、本地和链接分析筛选器 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 精简版查看器的首次发布 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 地图筛选器支持动态文本  |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 在优化故事体验中启用后台导出选项
    • 将优化故事导出至PowerPoint  |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 表 – 版本排序 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 实时 SAP BW 的基于动态时间的计算 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）（视频）
    • 变量支持 - 保存故事时不包括当前变量选择 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 将差异显示为瀑布图（适用于条形图/柱形图和水平瀑布图）|（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 故事计算
    • 交叉计算中的聚合
    • 从可用对象面板添加计算
  • 故事可扩展性
    • 自定义移动端的查看时工具栏 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 支持书签自定义微件 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 使用文本池支持脚本翻译 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 获取和设置无值成员的输入控制 API |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 用户协作
    • 在应用了基于角色的数据安全的模型中进行留言 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 在新模型的受限交叉计算中启用数据点留言 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 移除发布故事时“无结束日期”选项 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 在计划失败或部分成功时向用户发送通知 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 在表中使用维属性时，支持数据点留言 |（SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • “包含格式”选项现在对计划发布 CSV 格式可用

 

系统和服务管理

可信任域的外部超链接列表

用户现在可以在被重定向到外部 URL 时启用警告信息。管理员可以创建免于警告的可信任域列表。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

 

切换到 SAP Profitability and Performance Management 中的高级计算引擎

  • 使用外壳栏导航菜单右上角的产品切换器菜单在 SAP分析云和 SAP Profitability and Performance Management 之间导航
  • 登录一次后无需重新验证即可再次访问服务

注：在 SAP 分析云的切换菜单中选择高级计算引擎时，外壳栏导航菜单会在新的浏览器选项卡中打开，显示 SAP Profitability and Performance Management 的入口页面。其 URL 定义在租户的系统管理页面的 "链接我的租户 "部分。 （SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

业务内容交付

用于共识净收入规划、财务和销售规划以及流程洞察的业务内容包

通过采用全新的预构建数据模型和故事，缩短实施 SAP 分析云所需的时间。

2024 年第一季度更新的行业和业务领域内容包包括：（SAP 路线图浏览器）

  • 新的行业和业务领域内容包：
    • 安全建议仪表板
    • SAP S/4HANA 投资组合财务规划
    • 共识净收入规划
    • 战略性劳动力规划
  • 更新的行业和业务领域内容包：
    • SAP S/4HANA 云计算的公共部门管理 KPI 报告
    • 公共部门管理规划
    • 商业规划
    • SAP S/4HANA 集成财务规划的跨模型功能
    • SAP S/4HANA 集团财务规划
    • SAP S/4HANA 集成财务规划
    • 扩展规划与分析 (XP&A) 和人力经验管理 (HXM)
    • 针对 SAP S/4HANA 和 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 的企业资产管理和工厂维护分析

点击此处了解有关 SAP Analytics 业务内容的更多信息。

 

数据中心可用性

数据中心位于瑞士，可通过 Microsoft Azure 接入欧盟网络

通过支持在瑞士的 Microsoft Azure 上部署，客户现在在数据中心位置和超级分销商平台方面有了更多选择。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

数据集成

数据导出 API - 支持公共维

  • 添加一个新的命名空间以显示公共维
  • 支持two-legged 和 three-legged OAuth 协议

注：适用于 Cloud Foundry 租户。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

企业计划

数据操作 - 以分析模型为源进行跨模型复制

使用跨模型复制操作时，能够将 SAP 分析云的分析（非规划）模型作为源模型读取数据。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

 

 

Microsoft Office 集成

Microsoft Excel 中的成员选择器的持续更新 - 选择范围

Excel 中扩展的成员选择器功能包括：

  • 选择一系列成员，将其定义为一个过滤器

只有在成员选择器中以 "平面列表模式 "显示成员时，该功能才可用。（SAP 路线图浏览器）

请注意，这只是预览版，将于 2024 年第一季度晚些时候发布。

 

在 Microsoft Excel 中设置静态报告的格式

  • 为表格提供新选项，允许用户停用默认表格格式并应用 Excel 样式
  • 停用默认表格格式时，用户可为插件表格添加 Excel 格式，并在执行 "刷新 "操作时保持格式不变
  • 用户可为静态表格创建高度自定义的格式；但这种格式不支持展开和折叠操作

（SAP 路线图浏览器）

该功能自 2024 年 1 月 31 日起可用。

 

Microsoft Excel 设计器窗格标题的更新

  • 通过设计器窗格页眉显示工作表中所选对象的详细内容，使用户能够更改对象，如表格、数据操作触发器或用于公式的数据源。
  • 对标题进行额外更新，以丰富信息和导航功能：
    • 用工作表中显示的对象（表格、数据操作触发器或公式的数据源别名）的详细信息丰富标题中的信息

（SAP 路线图浏览器）

该功能自 2024 年 1 月 31 日起可用。

 

本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和增强功能的高级概述。有关这些功能的更多详细信息，请访问 SAP 分析云帮助门户。您还可以访问 SAP 路线图浏览器查看 SAP 分析云即将推出的功能。
